quotes.verbatim_quote:
Augmented chords repeat themselves every 4 frets the same way diminished chords repeat
every 3 frets
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes the augmented chord's 4-fret symmetry analogous to diminished's 3-fret.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
It is wise to think of the + chord
as a 7+ with no 57
quotes.why_load_bearing: Reframes augmented as dominant-derived (7#5 minus b7) for functional use.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Almost any dominant type chord can be moved in intervals of 3 frets as long as the LAST chord
resolves into wherever you are going next.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines the diminished-cycle dominant motion technique.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Dominant chords that have Sths and/or #5ths may be used in 2 FRET INTERVALS effectively,
still being careful to resolve nicely.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines whole-tone parallel dominant motion for altered chords.

quotes.topic: Minor-major-7 for dominant a half-step lower
quotes.verbatim_quote:
m# type chords can be used in place of dominant chords whose roots are 1 fret lower (like
Cm#/9 for B7). This will only work if the dominant chord is being used as a V7 or V7 function.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Specific substitution rule connecting m(maj7) chords to dominants.

quotes.verbatim_quote: The 1159 chords are usually used for the V7 chord of a key, but not often for others

In most popular songs, chords of the dominant family are used in certain common patterns. One
key for understanding these patterns is found in what is known as the cycle of 4ths (also called the
cycle of 5ths).
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces the cycle as the organizing structure of common-practice progressions.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
CHORDS WITH ROOTS
BASED ON THE CYCLE OF
quotes.why_load_bearing: Names the cycle-based-series concept central to the chapter.

quotes.verbatim_quote: changing to new keys is called MODULATING.

a smooth transition can be achieved if the new key is
preceded by a IIm7 V7 progression OF THAT NEW KEY.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core modulation technique: precede new tonic with its ii-V.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When dominant chords are used as the V7 of the key or THE V7 of a V7, this will be called using

the chord as a V7FUNCTION OR DOMINANT FUNCTION.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines the secondary-dominant concept underlying cycle progressions.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
COMMON TONES“on the top notes of the chords will

sometimes help smooth out the transitions.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Voice-leading aid: shared top notes ease key transitions.
